page_metadata.biotools__summary:
Three-dimensional virtual refocusing of fluorescence microscopy images using deep learning.

DeepZ Plugin Release_v1.3 – Google Drev.

Three-dimensional propagation and time-reversal of fluorescence images.

Neural network learns fluorescence wave propagation and time-reversal to propagate a 2D fluorescence image onto user-defined 3D surfaces, enabling 3D imaging of fluorescent samples using a single 2D image, without mechanical scanning, additional hardware, or a trade-off of resolution or speed. For details, refer to our publication "Three-dimensional propagation and time-reversal of fluorescence images" [1]. (Video below: 3D reconstruction of a C. elegans using Deep-Z inference)
